dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6799 IH: Bridging Relief in Delayed Government Enrollment for Young-Onset Alzheimer’s Disease Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Kim (for herself, Mr. Goldman of New York, Mr. Bacon, and Mrs. Foushee) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend title II of the Social Security Act to eliminate the waiting periods for disability insurance benefits and Medicare coverage for individuals with young-onset Alzheimer’s, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Bridging Relief in Delayed Government Enrollment for Young-Onset Alzheimer’s Disease Act of 2025 or the BRIDGE for Young-Onset Alzheimer’s Disease Act of 2025.

section.header: Elimination of waiting period for individuals with young-onset Alzheimer’s

subsection.text: Section 223(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 423(a)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), in the matter following subparagraph (E), by inserting or young-onset Alzheimer’s (as defined by the Commissioner through the Program Operations Manual System or a successor document) after amyotrophic lateral sclerosis; and
in paragraph (2)(B), by inserting or (iii) after clause (ii).

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to applications for disability insurance benefits filed after the date that is 5 months before the date of the enactment of this Act.

section.header: Waiver of 24-month waiting period for Medicare coverage of individuals with young-onset Alzheimers’s

subsection.text: Section 226(h) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 426(h)) is amended by inserting or young-onset Alzheimer’s (as defined by the Commissioner through the Program Operations Manual System or a successor document) after amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS).
The amendments made by this section shall apply to benefits for months beginning after the date of the enactment of this Act.
